summary: Linked `src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c` by switching it from NonMatching to Matching and making `ftCo_8008A7A8` reach 100%. The match was achieved with control-flow/codegen shaping in `ftwaitanim.c`, a manual inline of `ftCo_8008A6D8`, cleanup of wait-animation data typing, and symbol-table updates for newly linked data. decomp.dev reported `main/melee/ft/ftwaitanim` / `ftCo_8008A7A8` improved from 99.86% to 100%, with +592 matched code bytes, +864 linked code bytes, and +72 linked data bytes.

key_files.role: Primary matching work for `ftCo_8008A6D8`, `getAnimID`, and especially `ftCo_8008A7A8`; includes manual inline and assert macro change.
Renamed `struct S_TEMP4` to `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData`, typed its `xC` field as `union CmdUnion*`, updated `ftData` and `Fighter` fields, and removed `ftSubactionList`.
Updated local/cast types from `S_TEMP4` to `Fighter_WaitAnimData` for wait animation data access.
Adjusted local data/sdata/sdata2/BSS symbol names and scopes after linking.

what_changed: `ftCo_8008A7A8` was reshaped: the no-wait-data/item-held branch now stores `fp->anim_id` in a temporary, calls `ftCo_8008A6D8`, and returns early.
The randomized wait-animation selection path now uses `temp` and `anim_id` locals in the do/while condition, then manually inlines the body of `ftCo_8008A6D8` instead of calling it.
`ftCo_8008A6D8` now uses `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*` and assigns `anim->xC` directly to `fp->x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u` instead of casting from `ftSubactionList*`.
`getAnimID` replaced the explicit `OSReport(...); __assert("ftwaitanim.c", 86, "0")` sequence with `HSD_ASSERTREPORT(86, 0, "wait anim data illegal!!\n", max)`.
`struct S_TEMP4` was renamed to `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData`; its command-script pointer field changed from `ftSubactionList*` to `union CmdUnion*`.
`ftData`, `Fighter`, `fighter.c`, and `ftdata.c` were updated to use `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData*` consistently.
`struct ftSubactionList` and its forward typedef were removed.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` renamed several formerly semantic local symbols such as `efAlt_803BF9D0` and `efAlt_804D81D0` to compiler-style local labels like `@707`, `@702`, and added local labels such as `...data.0` and `...bss.0`.
smart_moves: The PR explicitly documents the matching workaround: `ftCo_8008A7A8` manually inlines `ftCo_8008A6D8` with a `@todo` note saying it was "not clean but only way I could make it match."
Using `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` captured the assert/report pattern at the macro level instead of forcing separate `OSReport` and `__assert` calls.
Changing `Fighter_WaitAnimData.xC` to `union CmdUnion*` removed a cast at the command-script assignment site and better matched the actual consumer type.
The type cleanup was propagated through `types.h`, `ftdata.c`, and `fighter.c` before marking the object Matching, reducing local casts and temporary placeholder names.
The symbol updates correctly treated many compiler-generated constants and jump/data labels as local `@NNN` or `...section.N` symbols, which is typical churn when an object becomes linked.
decomp_lessons: For tiny wrapper-like fighter code, a direct call to a helper may not match even when semantically correct; manually inlining the helper can be required to reproduce original codegen.

MWCC-sensitive locals matter: introducing a seemingly unused `enum_t temp`, preserving `anim_id`, and structuring a do/while around the exact temporary can affect register allocation and branch shape.
When an `OSReport` plus `__assert` sequence appears, check whether the original source likely used `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`; the macro may produce the expected layout more cleanly.
Replace placeholder struct names such as `S_TEMP4` when enough usage context exists. Here the data was used by wait animation selection and command-script setup, justifying `Fighter_WaitAnimData`.
If a struct field is always consumed as `union CmdUnion*`, type the field that way rather than preserving a dummy one-byte subaction-list struct and casting at every use.
Linking a previously nonmatching object can require broad symbol-file adjustments for local literals, jumptables, floats/doubles, and section labels; these are not necessarily behavioral changes.
naming_conventions: `struct S_TEMP4` was replaced with the domain-specific name `struct Fighter_WaitAnimData`.
`Fighter_WaitAnimData` follows the existing fighter-system style of prefixing shared fighter structs with `Fighter_` and describing their role.
The field name `x10_animCurrFlags` was retained, suggesting partial semantic knowledge while preserving offset-based naming for less-understood fields.
`ftSubactionList` was removed rather than retained as a misleading placeholder type.
Local compiler-generated data symbols in `symbols.txt` use `@NNN` with `scope:local`, while section labels use forms such as `...data.0` and `...bss.0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Manual inline of `ftCo_8008A6D8` inside `ftCo_8008A7A8` to match final codegen.
Early return after the `arg1 == NULL` / item-held branch to shape the surrounding control flow.
Preserved separate `temp` and `anim_id` locals in the wait-animation do/while path to match assignment and comparison behavior.
Used `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` for the illegal wait-animation-data assert/report path.
Typed `anim->xC` as `union CmdUnion*` to avoid an explicit cast in the matched assignment to `fp->x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u`.
Updated `configure.py` only after the unit was matching, moving `melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching`.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 60.82% (+0.02%, +592 bytes), linked code 33.47% (+0.02%, +864 bytes), linked data 32.23% (+0.01%, +72 bytes).
Reported one new match: `main/melee/ft/ftwaitanim` item `ftCo_8008A7A8` went from 99.86% to 100.00%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR dump; the only comment evidence is the automated decomp.dev report.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the manual inline in `ftCo_8008A7A8` or clean up the `@todo` in `ftwaitanim.c`.
Search for other uses of `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` replacing `OSReport` plus `__assert` in matched fighter code.
Search for additional placeholder struct renames from `S_TEMP*` to domain-specific `Fighter_*` names.
Look for subsequent PRs improving `Fighter_WaitAnimData` field names beyond `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, and `x14`.
Search symbol-file changes involving `...data.0`, `...bss.0`, and local `@NNN` labels when linking nonmatching objects.
